sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.18 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: Let$F$be a finitely generated non-Abelian free group and let$G$be the Cartesian (unrestricted) product of countable infinity of copies of$F$. Must the Abelianization$G/G'$of$G$be torsion-free? { A.\,M.\,Gaglione,~D.\,Spellman} No, it may contain elements of order~$2$\ (O.\,Kharlampovich, A.\,Myasnikov, in: Knots, braids, and mapping class groups\,---\,papers dedicated to Joan S.~Birman (New York, ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
